text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Whimbly, there lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He had a peculiar hobby: every evening after supper, Oliver would sit by his oak tree and read the ancient tomes he had collected over a century.

One night, he stumbled upon a forgotten legend about a brave young girl named Melody, who could hear the whispers of the forest. The story spoke of a forbidden flower, capable of revealing secrets.

Melody lived next door, tending to her grandmother's garden. Every so often, she could hear the owl’s voice. After an odd night listening to these whispers, she sought out Oliver.

"Why do you listen to these strange tales?" she asked.

Oliver chuckled softly.

"The secrets of the woods hold many wonders. And your voice echoes through my tree."

One day, Melody decided to pick the forbidden flower. As soon as she did, she felt her mind expand with knowledge.
